title: UK Age Appropriate Design Code (ICO Children's Code)
authority: ICO — UK GDPR / Data Protection Act 2018

rules.intent: The service must establish age with a level of certainty appropriate to the risks — a real age-assurance step at sign-up, not nothing.
rules.fix: Capture date of birth or run age assurance at registration so age-appropriate treatment can be applied.

rules.intent: A bare self-declaration checkbox ('I am over 18') is not sufficient age assurance under current ICO/Ofcom expectations.
rules.fix: Replace or back the self-declared age checkbox with real age assurance (DOB + verification, estimation, or ID).

rules.intent: Settings must be 'high privacy' by default for children — profiles private unless the child changes it.
rules.fix: Default profile visibility to private/followers-only for accounts that may belong to a child.

rules.intent: Geolocation must default to off for children, and any setting that makes a child's location visible to others must default off.
rules.fix: Default any location-sharing / geolocation setting to off; switch it back off at the end of each session.

rules.intent: Do not use nudge techniques to lead or encourage children to provide unnecessary personal data or weaken their privacy.
rules.fix: Remove copy/flows that push users to make profiles public, share location, or disable privacy to unlock features.

rules.intent: Where the service relies on consent for a child under 13, parental/guardian consent must be obtained.
rules.fix: Add a parental/guardian consent flow for under-13 sign-ups before processing their data.

rules.intent: A DPIA must be completed and maintained for services likely to be accessed by children.
rules.fix: Add and keep current a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) artifact in the repo or linked from it.
